sections.subtitle: Current Form
sections.text: Athletic Club has struggled recently, losing 4 of their last 5 matches with no goals scored in those losses, indicating a lack of attacking threat. Girona FC, on the other hand, is in dismal form, winless in their last 6 matches and conceding heavily (15 goals in 5 games). Both teams are defensively vulnerable, but Athletic Club has shown slightly better structure at home.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: In the last two head-to-head matches, results are split with Athletic Club winning 3-0 at home and Girona FC edging a 2-1 victory at home. Matches between these sides often see goals, with 6 of the last 7 having over 2.5 goals and both teams scoring in most encounters.

sections.subtitle: Season Statistics
sections.text: Athletic Club ranks mid-table (7th) with a balanced attack and defense (6 goals scored and conceded), showing strength in corners (7 per game) and final third entries. Girona FC, at the bottom (20th), has a poor defensive record (15 goals conceded) and struggles to convert chances, scoring only 2 goals this season.

sections.subtitle: Key Factors
sections.text: Athletic Club's home advantage at San Mamés and Girona's consistent inability to keep clean sheets (7 games without one) could be decisive. The referee's average of 4.7 yellow cards per game suggests a potentially feisty encounter, while Athletic's high corner count may lead to set-piece opportunities.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Half-time overview
halftimeSections.text: At half-time, Girona FC leads Athletic Club 1-0 at San Mamés in Bilbao. The away side struck early through Azzedine Ounahi in the 9th minute, capitalizing on a clinical move to take the lead. Athletic Club, despite dominating possession and territory, has been unable to convert their chances, leaving them trailing at the break. Both teams have shown resilience, with no cards issued so far, indicating a competitive but disciplined encounter.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Athletic Club's first-half performance
halftimeSections.text: Athletic Club has controlled much of the game, holding a significant share of the ball and creating numerous entries into the final third and penalty area. Their aerial dominance and ability to win duels have been notable, giving them an edge in physical battles. However, their finishing has let them down, with a big chance missed and limited conversion of shots on target. Players like Gorka Guruzeta and Oihan Sancet have shown promise in attack but need to be more clinical. Defensively, they've been solid, though they were caught out for Girona's goal.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Girona FC's first-half performance
halftimeSections.text: Girona FC has taken a more pragmatic approach, conceding possession but making the most of their limited opportunities. Their early goal from Ounahi has given them a crucial advantage, and they’ve created dangerous moments despite fewer entries into the final third. Defensively, they’ve struggled to win duels, especially in the air, but have managed to hold firm against Athletic Club’s pressure for the most part. Azzedine Ounahi has been the standout, not only scoring but also contributing creatively.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Attack momentum analysis
halftimeSections.text: The first half saw Athletic Club dominate the early stages with consistent attacking spells, pushing forward with intent in the opening minutes. However, momentum shifted briefly to Girona around the time of their goal, as they enjoyed a sustained period of threat. Following the goal, Athletic Club regained control, mounting several waves of pressure and maintaining a high level of intensity in attack for much of the half. Towards the end of the half, Girona showed brief signs of counter-attacking intent, but Athletic Club largely kept the upper hand in terms of forward momentum.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Second-half outlook
halftimeSections.text: Looking ahead to the second half, Athletic Club is likely to continue their aggressive approach, leveraging their home advantage at San Mamés to push for an equalizer. Their ability to create chances in the first half suggests they will remain a threat, especially if they can sharpen their finishing. Girona FC, on the other hand, will probably focus on defending their lead, looking to exploit counter-attacking opportunities while soaking up pressure. Substitutions could play a key role, particularly for Athletic Club, who may introduce fresh legs to break down Girona’s defense.
